# DryUI — Agent Install Instructions

Zero-dependency Svelte 5 component library. Use the CLI to detect the project
and execute a tailored install plan.

## Step 1 — Run the install planner

```
npx @dryui/cli install --json
```

This detects the project's framework, package manager, theme state, and returns
a JSON install plan. The response is an object with:

- `detection` — project info (framework, packageManager, files, dependencies, theme state)
- `steps` — ordered array of actions to execute

Each step has:
- `kind`: `"install-package"` | `"edit-file"` | `"create-file"` | `"note"` | `"blocked"`
- `status`: `"pending"` | `"done"` | `"blocked"`
- `title` and `description`
- `command` (for install-package steps) — run this shell command
- `path` and `snippet` (for edit-file / create-file steps) — apply the snippet to the file

Execute each `"pending"` step in order:
- `install-package` → run the `command` (e.g. `npm install @dryui/ui`)
- `create-file` → create the file at `path` with the `snippet` as content
- `edit-file` → add or merge the `snippet` into the existing file at `path`
- `note` / `blocked` → read the `description` for guidance

If all steps have `status: "done"`, the project is already set up.

## Step 2 — Set up the MCP server (recommended)

Add the DryUI MCP server to the project's `.mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dryui": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

This gives you tools for component lookup, composition guidance, code review,
theme diagnosis, and workspace health checks.

## Step 3 — Use components

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button } from '@dryui/ui';
</script>

<Button>Click me</Button>
```

## Full API reference

See https://dryui.dev/llms.txt for the complete component catalog, props,
theming tokens, and usage examples.
